_doc: Monotone-shrink ratchet budget for the conformance matrix. Each key is the exact suite name emitted by conformance-matrix.py; `ledgered` is the allowed count of ledgered gaps (the XFail/Skip column). The gate asserts the live count EQUALS this budget: a larger count (a regressed or newly-ledgered gap) fails RED, and a smaller count (a fixed gap) also fails RED until the budget is lowered here to lock the gain. This budget is authoritative and may only ever be edited DOWNWARD; a raise must be justified in review. Reasons describe the technical gap only.

SPARQL 1.1/1.2 evaluation (full corpus).note: 5 upstream-errata fixtures with non-canonical XSD lexicals (cast-decimal/double/float, coalesce01, plus-1-corrected); the entailment-regime cases (OWL-Direct/DL via the ALCOIQ tableau, RIF-rule, RDF-axiomatic-triple) are all native now

rdflib LSP drop-in gate.note: rdflib-suite strict xfails: Graph-subclass set-operator identity, rdf:List/Collection mutation, Result.bindings/SELECT* subselect projection, graph-prefix forwarding, aggregate/nested-FILTER evaluation, and ConjunctiveGraph legacy semantics

purrdf.compat parity.note: purrdf.compat differential strict xfails vs rdflib 7.6
